description: Narrative claims Crosscheck makes about itself that cannot be verified by reference/structural integrity alone. Each records the claim, the observed reality, a review status, and (where possible) an auto-check that re-fires if reality changes. status='unreviewed' fails CI to force triage.

narrative_claims.claim: Six layers of correctness checks, four shipping today.
narrative_claims.reality: Layers 1,4,5,6 have shipping skills; Layers 2 (compilation correctness) and 3 (contract-graph) do not. README discloses this honestly ('four of them are shipping today').

narrative_claims.claim: add-orchestrator drives spec -> approved invariant docs READY FOR IMPLEMENTATION.
narrative_claims.reality: Accurate. The agent explicitly stops at the routing recommendation and disclaims implementation-correctness closure. NOT drift.

narrative_claims.claim: ADD includes a Phase 4 gated-implementation loop that drives code to green within three legal commit shapes.
narrative_claims.reality: Shipped as a spec. agents/lowry.md specifies the Phase 4 run-to-green loop: entry contract, the gate bundle, the three-shape commit classifier (A4's ClassifyCommitShape, now implemented in conformance/acceptance/oracles.go and passing), the single batched human interrupt, the defer path, the two-tier completion contract (passes-oracles != matches-intent), and the A5 drift-stop kill criterion (stop + emit a drift packet rather than weaken an invariant). Resolved design decisions are in docs/add/phase4-design-decisions.md, grounded in the ngst greenfield run whose cli module was a hand-run of this loop. A4 is mechanically enforceable today; the judged acceptance oracles (A1/A2/A5/A6) await their scenario-runner + LLM-judge harness, so the run-to-green behaviour is specified but not yet harness-validated end-to-end.

narrative_claims.claim: Three operating modes (bootstrap / add / transitional) with module-level mode tags honoured by skills.
narrative_claims.reality: Wired in v1. The three modes and the module-level add-mode tag are documented in docs/add/operating-modes.md; every crosscheck skill/agent now declares add-mode, enforced in the blocking conformance oracle (AUTO 6) — the A3 acceptance oracle's deterministic pass condition. add-orchestrator Step 0 selects a mode from repo state and routes three entrypoints: add/spec-consult (consume the spec, do NOT cold-elicit — the #150 gate from the ngst greenfield run), bootstrap/legacy-derive (invariants from code), and greenfield/intent-elicit (assurance-init seed; the dedicated ADR-004 S2.1-S2.4 greenfield skills and full per-skill mode-aware branching are incremental follow-ups tracked on #219).

narrative_claims.claim: The canonical ADD methodology (methodology.md, glossary.md, the ADRs) lives in the repo.
narrative_claims.reality: Deliberately NOT committed, by design — decision recorded under epic #217: do not promote a methodology doc yet. v1 (methodology.md, glossary.md, intent.md, the six ADRs) was retracted on 2026-05-11 and moved under docs/add/.retrospective/v1-archive/ after it reproduced its own failure mode (docs/add/JOURNAL.md). docs/add/README.md states there is no canonical methodology doc here, by design. Of the two README preconditions, the mature-repo run has landed (2026-05-15, docs/add/JOURNAL.md); the greenfield run is still pending (tracked in #231). Either way its automation is mid-flight (Phase-4 #218, modes #219, auditor #220). A canonical methodology should FOLLOW that automation, not lead it: committing one now would document a methodology whose back half is unbuilt, reintroducing the plausible!=correct gap this oracle exists to catch. The ratified A1-A6 acceptance oracles (conformance/acceptance/) are the current authoritative contract; the design rationale lives in the archived ADRs. The doc gets written to describe what shipped once the #217 children land.

narrative_claims.claim: A Phase 5 Auditor agent renders settled/active/drifted verdicts on every artifact.
narrative_claims.reality: Shipped as a spec. agents/auditor.md specifies the Phase 5 read-only consolidation agent: the settled/active/drifted verdict taxonomy with exact evidence criteria, the deterministic-signals-detect + LLM-renders-judgment split (ADR-002), the signals it consumes (coverage matrices, session-marker hashes, JOURNAL diff-types, git recency, linkage orphans, mode tags), its zero-write-authority-outside-its-report contract, mode-appropriate governance, and cadence. It is a distinct peer (ADR-003) so the authoring agent never audits its own artifact — the cure for the ngst #150 referential-integrity failure that escaped because nothing compared invariants to the spec post-draft. Honest reach: (1) the read-only contract is PROSE-asserted, not harness-enforced — the agent frontmatter format carries no tools: allowlist yet (no agent in this repo has one), so the M4-auditor I3/F4.5 spawn-time enforcement is a behavioral contract, not a sandboxed capability; (2) the shipped taxonomy is 3-valued (settled/active/drifted) — the M4 spec's 4th verdict ActiveWithWarning is deliberately collapsed into active; (3) several drift criteria key off frontmatter (consumes:, add-mode) and an attestation timestamp the current docs/invariants/*.md targets do not carry, so on those artifacts those criteria pass vacuously and a settled verdict attests only 'no signal computable' — the agent discloses this rather than silently emitting a confident settled; (4) the cited ADR-002/003/005 are pending commit (#217), restated inline; (5) a unified instrumentation script and the judged-oracle harness do not exist yet; today the signals are assembled from the conformance oracle + git + session artifacts, and the prose-vs-prose intent verdict is LLM judgment to spot-check.

narrative_claims.claim: Crosscheck applies its own assurance machinery to itself.
narrative_claims.reality: Growing — narrowed from known-gap. docs/invariants/ verifies three leaf helpers via Dafny (parseDafnyOutput, shouldExclude, extractDifficultyMetrics). Trunk coverage now has two mechanical self-checks: (1) this conformance/inventory oracle (docs<->artifacts), and (2) the AUTO 5 orchestration-graph integrity check (every skill/agent an orchestrator routes to via a backtick `/x` or `/crosscheck:x` token in its body must resolve to a real artifact). What AUTO 5 does NOT catch: bare-slash routing (`/skill` without backticks) is invisible, so a skill routed to only via bare-slash is unchecked (false negative); a non-crosscheck backtick `/token` in an agent body is flagged even when it names a sibling-plugin skill, an example, or a flag (false positive, no cross-plugin allowlist); and plain-prose / bare-word agent->agent edges (the dominant form, e.g. auditor.md routing to byfuglien/hellebuyck/lowry in prose) are not modelled at all. These are tracked in #221 as the trunk-coverage epic. Gate-semantics and full-workflow self-checks remain partial; #221 stays open to grow trunk coverage toward reviewed-accurate.
